2604.13452.title: CANVAS: Continuity-Aware Narratives via Visual Agentic Storyboarding
2604.13452.paper_summary: Long-form visual storytelling requires maintaining continuity across shots, including consistent characters, stable environments, and smooth scene transitions. While existing generative models can produce strong individual frames, they fail to preserve such continuity, leading to appearance changes, inconsistent backgrounds, and abrupt scene shifts. We introduce CANVAS (Continuity-Aware Narratives via Visual Agentic Storyboarding), a multi-agent framework that explicitly plans visual continuity in multi-shot narratives. CANVAS enforces coherence through character continuity, persistent background anchors, and location-aware scene planning for smooth transitions within the same setting We evaluate CANVAS on two storyboard generation benchmarks ST-BENCH and ViStoryBench and introduce a new challenging benchmark HardContinuityBench for long-range narrative consistency. CANVAS consistently outperforms the best-performing baseline, improving background continuity by 21.6%, character consistency by 9.6% and props consistency by 7.6%.
2604.13452.paper_summary_zh: 長篇視覺敘事需要在鏡頭之間保持連貫性，包括一致的角色、穩定的環境和流暢的場景轉換。儘管現有的生成模型可以產生強大的單獨畫面，但它們無法保持這種連貫性，導致外觀變化、不一致的背景和突兀的場景轉換。我們介紹了CANVAS（透過視覺代理故事板的連貫性意識敘事），這是一個多代理框架，明確規劃多鏡頭敘事中的視覺連貫性。CANVAS通過角色連貫性、持續的背景錨點和位置感知的場景規劃來強化一致性，以實現同一環境中的流暢轉換。我們在兩個故事板生成基準ST-BENCH和ViStoryBench上評估CANVAS，並引入了一個新的挑戰性基準HardContinuityBench，以測試長距離敘事的一致性。CANVAS在表現上始終超越最佳基準，背景連貫性提高了21.6%，角色一致性提高了9.6%，道具一致性提高了7.6%。
2604.13452.author: Ishani Mondal et.al.
2604.13452.authors: Ishani Mondal, Yiwen Song, Mihir Parmar, Palash Goyal, Jordan Boyd-Graber, Tomas Pfister, Yale Song
